title: Refresher B - Class Manager App
source: RMS flexiquiz_exam_results.response_questions_json latest submitted payload per mini quiz

questions.text: Your patient is a 25-year-old female with a history of diabetes. She is confused, agitated, and verbally abusive to you, and she is very sweaty. Although she refuses to give a history of the present illness, you should suspect which of the following as the likely cause of the patient's presentation?

options.label: C
options.text: Excessive intake of foods high in sugar, such as soda or candy

options.label: D
options.text: A nondiabetic-related problem, such as a head injury or mental illness

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Failure to intake sufficient sugar. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: Your patient is a 25-year-old female with a history of diabetes. She is confused, agitated, and verbally abusive to you, and she is very sweaty. Although she refuses to give a history of the present illness, you should suspect which of the following as the likely cause of the patient's presentation. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

The patient is pale and has been weak for the past few days. She has:

questions.text: Compared to hypoglycemia, which of the following is true of hyperglycemia?

options.label: C
options.text: It is more easily treated in the prehospital environment than hypoglycemia.

options.label: D
options.text: Its onset is preceded by an aura, such as hallucinations or detecting unusual odors.

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Its onset is more gradual.. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: Compared to hypoglycemia, which of the following is true of hyperglycemia. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: The EMT, after administering any medication, must do which of the following?

options.label: A
options.text: Document the administration, reassess the patient, and report to the receiving facility.

options.label: B
options.text: Document the route, dose, and time; reassess the patient; and re-administer the medication.

options.label: C
options.text: Wait 5 minutes, repeat the medication if needed.

options.label: D
options.text: Reconsider the five rights, reassess the patient, and contact medical control.

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Document the administration, reassess the patient, and report to the receiving facility.. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: The EMT, after administering any medication, must do which of the following. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: As you prepare to enter a patient's room in a nursing home, you see a sign posted on the door indicating that the patient has a latex allergy. Which of the following is the BEST way to care for the patient?

options.label: A
options.text: Don synthetic exam gloves prior to making contact with this patient.

options.label: B
options.text: Put on latex gloves anyway; you must protect yourself with BSI.

options.label: C
options.text: Only perform a history on this patient, not a physical exam, so you can avoid contacting him with your gloves.

options.label: D
options.text: Ask the nursing staff if the patient has an epinephrine auto-injector available in case of a reaction to your gloves.

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Don synthetic exam gloves prior to making contact with this patient.. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: As you prepare to enter a patient's room in a nursing home, you see a sign posted on the door indicating that the patient has a latex allergy. Which of the following is the BEST way to care for the patient. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: You are called to a farmhouse on a sunny spring afternoon. Upon arrival, a frantic mother tells you that her 3-year-old daughter was playing in the yard, accidentally stepped on a hornets' nest, and was stung repeatedly. The patient is alert, screaming and crying, and can follow her mother's commands. Her arms and legs are swollen and show the marks of several stings. Her body is covered in hives. Her vital signs are blood pressure 90/40, pulse 110, respiratory rate 24, and oxygen saturation 99%. Her lung sounds are clear and equal bilaterally. Her mother states she put "nox-a-sting" on the bites but the bites only seemed to get worse. What condition is the patient suffering from?

options.label: A
options.text: Allergic reaction from the hornets' stings

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Allergic reaction from the hornets' stings. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: You are called to a farmhouse on a sunny spring afternoon. questions.text: You have arrived at the side of a 35-year-old female who is 37 weeks pregnant and in labor. Your exam reveals crowning with contractions 60 seconds apart. Your immediate action would be to:

options.label: A
options.text: Place the patient on her back, and then open and prepare the OB kit

options.label: B
options.text: Obtain vital signs, and then move the patient to the ambulance for a nonemergency transport

options.label: C
options.text: Place the patient on the cot in the left lateral recumbent position, and then proceed with emergency transport

options.label: D
options.text: Have the patient hold her legs together, and then move her to the ambulance for delivery of the baby

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Place the patient on her back, and then open and prepare the OB kit. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: You have arrived at the side of a 35-year-old female who is 37 weeks pregnant and in labor. questions.text: As you are assessing a 32-year-old asthmatic woman who has called EMS due to difficulty breathing, her husband enters the home through a back door and shouts, "Get your hands off her; she doesn't need your help. She needs to be taught a lesson about her smart mouth." Which of the following is the best course of action?

options.label: A
options.text: Leave the scene and patient, then notify police.

options.label: B
options.text: Let the husband know that his behavior is inappropriate, and if it continues you will call for the police.

options.label: C
options.text: Continue assessment and respond that the patient is sick and needs medical attention.

options.label: D
options.text: Attempt to remove the patient from the home and continue treatment en route to thehospital.

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Leave the scene and patient, then notify police.. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: As you are assessing a 32-year-old asthmatic woman who has called EMS due to difficulty breathing, her husband enters the home through a back door and shouts, "Get your hands off her; she doesn't need your help. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: A female patient has acute onset of right-sided abdominal pain, describes it as sharp, and rates it as 9/10. The primary survey reveals no life threats, but her skin is cool and diaphoretic. She states that she has been going to the bathroom more and is worried because her father has diabetes. Her last period was nine weeks ago, and she has a history of irregular periods. Her heart rate is 122 beats/min, respirations are 20 breaths/min, blood pressure is 138/68 mmHg, and SaO2 is 97%. In this scenario, which bit of information is of most concern to the EMT?
questions.feedback: Correct answer: Last period nine weeks ago. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: A female patient has acute onset of right-sided abdominal pain, describes it as sharp, and rates it as 9/10. questions.text: Fill in the missing information, in the highlighted section, from the choices below Epinephrine 1:1,000 (EpiPen, EpiPen Jr.) Class Vasoconstrictor & Bronchodilator Action Indications Signs & Symptoms of Anaphylactic Shock Contraindications None when the indications and 5 Rights are present Dose 0.3 mg – adult EpiPen 0.15 mg – EpiPen Jr. Route Intramuscular (lateral thigh) Side Effects Increased heart rate, increased blood pressure, anxiety, headache, nausea/vomiting

options.label: A
options.text: Constricts blood vessels, raising blood pressure & dilates bronchi, reducing respiratory distress

options.label: B
options.text: Binds to opiate receptors and blocks the opiate from entering the cell

options.label: C
options.text: Dilates the coronary vessels, reduces pre-load

options.label: E
options.text: Binds to red blood cells, provides perfusion to all cells of the body

options.label: F
options.text: Reduces clotting ability of blood, prevents and treats clots associated with myocardial infarction

options.label: G
options.text: Absorbs some poisons (bind them to the surface of the charcoal) to prevent them from being absorbed by the body

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Constricts blood vessels, raising blood pressure & dilates bronchi, reducing respiratory distress. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: Fill in the missing information, in the highlighted section, from the choices below Epinephrine 1:1,000 (EpiPen, EpiPen Jr.) Class Vasoconstrictor & Bronchodilator Action Indications Signs & Symptoms of Anaphylactic Shock Contraindications None when the indications and 5 Rights are present Dose 0.3 mg – adult EpiPen 0.15 mg – EpiPen Jr. Route Intramuscular (lateral thigh) Side Effects Increased heart rate, increased blood pressure, anxiety, headache, nausea/vomiting. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: You are by the side of a patient who complains of severe chest pain that radiates into his right arm and neck. He is alert and oriented, with an open airway and adequate breathing. His radial pulse is strong, and his skin is cool and diaphoretic. Your partner reports his pulse rate is 84, respirations are 18 breaths/min, blood pressure is 86/62 mmHg, and SpO2 is 98% on room air. You have obtained a medical history and performed the secondary assessment. The patient states that he has had two heart attacks in the past and is allergic to aspirin and sulfa medications. What should you do next?

options.text: Administer oxygen and nitroglycerin, and then transport the patient

options.text: Administer baby aspirin to the patient

questions.text: Which of the following is the cause of most strokes?

options.label: A
options.text: Blockage of an artery supplying part of the brain

options.label: B
options.text: A ruptured cerebral artery due to hypertension

options.label: C
options.text: A ruptured cerebral artery due to an aneurysm

options.label: D
options.text: A spasm in an artery supplying part of the brain

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Blockage of an artery supplying part of the brain. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: Which of the following is the cause of most strokes. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: What is a Large Vessel Occlusion (LVO) stroke?

options.label: A
options.text: An ischemic stroke caused by a blockage in one of the major arteries of the brain

options.label: B
options.text: An ischemic stroke caused by a blockage in the deep veins of the leg

options.label: C
options.text: A hemorrhagic stroke caused by a blockage in one of the major arteries of the brain

options.label: D
options.text: A hemorrhagic stroke caused by blunt force trauma to the skull

questions.feedback: Correct answer: An ischemic stroke caused by a blockage in one of the major arteries of the brain. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: What is a Large Vessel Occlusion (LVO) stroke. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: How can EMS identify a potential LVO patient in the field?

options.label: A
options.text: Rapid Arterial Occlusion Evaluation greater than or equal to 5

options.label: B
options.text: Rapid Arterial Occlusion Evaluation less than 5

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Rapid Arterial Occlusion Evaluation greater than or equal to 5. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: How can EMS identify a potential LVO patient in the field. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: There are two main types of dialysis, hemodialysis and peritoneal dialysis. The main difference between them is hemodialysis:

options.label: A
options.text: is usually done at a special facility and peritoneal dialysis is usually done at home.

options.label: B
options.text: is usually done at home and peritoneal dialysis is usually done at a special facility.

options.label: C
options.text: filters the urine and peritoneal dialysis filters the blood.

options.label: D
options.text: filters the blood and peritoneal dialysis filters the urine.

questions.feedback: Correct answer: is usually done at a special facility and peritoneal dialysis is usually done at home.. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: There are two main types of dialysis, hemodialysis and peritoneal dialysis. The main difference between them is hemodialysis.
